data.description:
## Why

Phase 2a swapped the CI Module-size gate to an absolute cap + admin-locked limit that reads NEITHER the allowlist nor the ceilings file. Those two files are now orphaned (empty of module entries, read by nothing). Delete them and prune the docs that describe the retired escape hatch. This must land AFTER Phase 2a (5f9d) so main's gate has already stopped reading the files — otherwise main's gate would break on their absence on this change's CI.

Also fixes two stale comments left by the reconcile split (ticket 3c7f): they still say route_inbound_probe 'stays' in reconcile.py, but it moved to reconcile_helpers.py (re-exported). Closing 3c7f is blocked on these.

## What

1. `git rm` `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt`.
2. `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py`: add a test asserting the allowlist + ceilings files no longer exist (the escape hatch is gone for good).
3. `docs/architecture.md`: delete the '### Current offenders (> 800 LOC) and planned remedy' section entirely (its table now holds only a stale `attest.py` row); update the '## Module-size policy' prose to describe the ABSOLUTE 800 cap (no allowlist), the single-source `.github/module-size-limit.txt`, and that changing the limit requires an admin to override the CI gate.
4. `AGENTS.md`: update the module-size policy paragraph — remove the `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` reference; state the cap is absolute and the limit is admin-locked.
5. Incidental (unblocks 3c7f): update the two stale residency comments so they say route_inbound_probe now lives in `reconcile_helpers.py` and is re-exported —
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py` (the `route_inbound_probe stays here` comment)
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py` (the `reconcile.py keeps` comment)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` no longer exist (verify: `! test -e .github/module-size-allowlist.txt && ! test -e .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`)
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py` asserts both files are absent and passes (verify: `pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q`)
- [ ] The docs/architecture.md offenders table section is gone and no module-size-allowlist reference remains in the doc (verify: `! grep -q 'Current offenders' docs/architecture.md && ! grep -q 'module-size-allowlist' docs/architecture.md`)
- [ ] AGENTS.md no longer references the allowlist and states the cap is absolute (verify: `! grep -q 'module-size-allowlist' AGENTS.md`)
- [ ] The two stale reconcile residency comments are replaced (verify: `! grep -q 'route_inbound_probe stays here' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py && ! grep -q 'reconcile.py keeps' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py`)
- [ ] The full unit suite is green — no test/reference still expects the deleted files (verify: `pytest tests/unit -q -k 'module_size or ci_workflow_parity'`)

## Notes
Mechanical deletion + doc/comment edits; no runtime code logic changes. E5 testing-plan: the contract test (updated) is the oracle for the deletion; the doc/comment edits are verified by the greps in the ACs. Lands via the normal Gerrit flow (the new gate does not read these files, so deleting them is green).
data.title: Phase 2b: delete the drained allowlist/ceilings files + prune module-size docs
